nodes.context: Irish republican paramilitary group, continuation of Real IRA, formed in 2012.
Irish republican paramilitary group that preceded the New IRA.
Irish republican militant group that merged with Real IRA to form New IRA.
Irish republican paramilitary group, allied with New IRA.
Major crime gang involved in feud with New IRA member Michael Barr.
Political wing of the New IRA.

edges.evidence_quote: It is a continuation of the Real Irish Republican Army (Real IRA), which began to be called the 'New IRA' in July 2012 when Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups merged with it.
edges.context: New IRA is a continuation of Real IRA after merger with RAAD.

edges.evidence_quote: It is a continuation of the Real Irish Republican Army (Real IRA), which began to be called the 'New IRA' in July 2012 when Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups merged with it.
edges.context: RAAD merged with Real IRA to form New IRA.
Continuity IRA is listed as an ally of New IRA in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Gardaí suspected Barr was shot dead because it was believed by the Kinahan cartel that he had provided a 'safe house' to one of the gunmen in the Regency Hotel attack.
edges.context: Kinahan cartel killed a New IRA member, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: It is widely considered that Saoradh is the political wing of the New IRA.
edges.context: Saoradh is the political wing of New IRA.
